Package Options

Source: vignettes/package-options.Rmd
package-options.Rmd

The package has a number of options that can be set via System Environmental variables. You usually don’t need to change these options, and most of them are actually there to help contributors in developing the package.

How to change these options.

You can change a System Environmental Variable from within R with Sys.setenv() and get a set value with Sys.getenv(). To set variables more permanently you can use the .Renviron file, which can be easily changed with usethis::edit_r_environ().

Options overview

The following options are available:

Login credentials

See Set Up Your ETN Username and Password. These variables allow you to set your etn database username and password.

  • ETN_USER : Username to the etn database
  • ETN_PWD : Password to the etn database

OpenCPU Options

  • ETN_PROTOCOL : The package automatically switches between using a local connection to the etn database or the OpenCPU API depending on the availability of a local database connection is available. You can force the package to always use one or the other by setting this option to opencpu or localdb.
  • ETN_TEST_API : DANGER Overwrite the url where the etn OpenCPU API is hosted. This variable is used to switch the entire package over to a different url, for example the testing api. Changing this variable to anything other than the testing api url will break the package.

On this page

Developed by Pieter Huybrechts, Peter Desmet, Damiano Oldoni, Stijn Van Hoey, Research Foundation - Flanders.
